task_completion.description: Build interior partition walls to enclose a supervisor office, a staff break room, and restrooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view, new interior walls should be drawn to create enclosed spaces designated for an office, break room, and restrooms.

task_completion.description: Define named room zones or use text labels for at least 4 key areas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels or room names (e.g., Receiving, Bulk Storage, Pick & Pack, Shipping, Office, Break Room) must be visible in the 2D plan view for at least 4 distinct areas.

task_completion.description: Furnish the warehouse with at least 10 storage racking units arranged in rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 10 storage racks or shelving units must be visible in the 2D plan or 3D view, arranged in linear rows within the main floor area.

task_completion.description: Furnish the warehouse with at least 4 desks or tables for workstations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 desks or tables must be placed in the layout, visible in either the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Furnish the warehouse with at least 4 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 chairs must be placed in the layout, typically near the desks or in the break room.

task_completion.description: Equip the break room with a table and chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the enclosed rooms (the break room) must specifically contain at least one table and accompanying chairs.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application window, catalog, and toolbars being actively used, rather than terminal windows or text editors modifying the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: The agent worked within the provided starter file structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The underlying floor plan should show the pre-existing 3-level villa structure (with its original 48 structural walls), rather than a completely blank new project or a simple rectangular shell.

integrity.description: Furniture and walls were placed in a manner consistent with human GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: Items should appear to be dragged and dropped from the catalog, with selection boxes, rotation indicators, or dimension lines visible during the process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Note a critical discrepancy in the prompt: while the task description claims the starter file is an 'open 200 m² building shell', the actual starter file (warehouse_starter.sh3d) is a ~400 m² 3-level contemporary villa with 19 pre-existing rooms and 48 structural walls left intact (but stripped of furniture, doors, and windows). The agent must work within this existing villa structure to complete the task. To pass (threshold 70/100), the agent must create new walls, define at least 4 room labels, place at least 10 storage racks in rows, and place at least 4 desks/tables and 4 chairs.
